summary: Advanced Yoshi init decompilation by matching `melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_Init.c` to 85.02% for a 2.38 KB file. The main source work replaced a `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` body for `ftYs_Init_8012BDA0`, added `ftYs_Init_8012BE3C`, corrected `ftYs_Unk1_803B75C0` from a `Vec4`/0x10 rodata object to a `Vec3`/0xC float object, and adjusted declarations/includes needed for collision, effects, parts, and matrix vector types. Large asm and scratches updates reflect newly matched Yoshi init functions and address/label normalization. No review discussion was present in the slice.
what_changed: `ftYs_Init_8012BDA0` changed from `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` to C using `GET_FIGHTER`, stack padding, `ftColl_8007B0C0(gobj, Intangible)`, a local `struct UNK_SAMUS_S2`, `fp->ft_data->x8->x11`, repeated `FtPart_TransN`, `ftYs_Unk1_803B75C0`, scale `1`, and `ftColl_8007B5AC(fp, fp->hurt_capsules, &foo)`.

Added `ftYs_Init_8012BE3C`, which calls `ftParts_80074B0C(gobj, 0, 0)`, resets collision with `ftColl_8007B0C0(gobj, 0)`, takes `&fp->co_attrs.xBC`, resolves a part with `ftParts_8007500C(fp, 4)`, gets its joint, and spawns effect `1231` via `efAsync_Spawn(gobj, &fp1->x60C, 4, 1231, jobj, xBC)`.
Added includes to `ftYs_Init.c`: `<baselib/forward.h>`, `ef/efasync.h`, and `ft/ftcoll.h` to support the newly decompiled code.
Corrected `ftYs_Unk1_803B75C0` in `ftYs_SpecialN.c` from `Vec4 const` to `Vec3 const`, and declared it in `ftYs_SpecialN.h` with `<dolphin/mtx/types.h>` included.
Updated `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` so `ftYs_Unk1_803B75C0` is rodata size `0xC` with `data:float` instead of size `0x10` with `data:4byte`.

Regenerated or refreshed Yoshi init/special-N asm slices; `ftYs_Init.s` labels and comments shifted from local scratch offsets like `0x00000000` and `.L_000000F8` to real addresses like `0x8012B6E8` and `.L_8012B7E0`.

Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` around many Yoshi/Samus scratch-status lines, including OK entries for `func_8012B6E8`, `func_8012B804`, `func_8012B918`, `ftYoshi_OnDeath`, `func_8012BDA0`, and `func_8012BE3C`.
Added `.gitkeep` files under progress-site external asset directories.
smart_moves: The rodata type correction for `ftYs_Unk1_803B75C0` was central: making it a `Vec3` and marking the symbol as a 0xC float object aligned the source declaration, header extern, and symbol metadata with the code that assigns it into `foo.vec1` and `foo.vec2`.
Used an existing shared-looking `struct UNK_SAMUS_S2` rather than inventing a Yoshi-specific struct in this PR, which likely helped match layout-sensitive stack code while keeping the change minimal.
Preserved compiler-sensitive stack layout with explicit `PAD_STACK(4 * 2)` and nested `PAD_STACK(4 * 4)` around the local struct construction in `ftYs_Init_8012BDA0`.
Kept the unresolved inline issue in `ftYs_Init_8012BE3C` explicit with `/// @todo Some kind of inline here.` instead of hiding uncertainty behind overconfident names or abstractions.
Matched effect/collision/part interactions by threading existing project APIs together: `ftParts_80074B0C`, `ftColl_8007B0C0`, `ftParts_8007500C`, and `efAsync_Spawn`.
decomp_lessons: When a function constructs a temporary effect/collision descriptor on the stack, exact local scope and padding can be as important as the field assignments themselves.
Small rodata dimension mistakes can block matching: a zero vector used as a `Vec3` must be declared and symbolized as 0xC float data, not as a 0x10 `Vec4` placeholder.

If a matched function reuses an existing unknown struct from another fighter/module, it can be acceptable as an interim decomp tactic when the binary layout is the evidence and no better type name is available.
For fighter init code, decomp often crosses subsystem boundaries: parts visibility/state, hurt/collision state, common attributes, joints, and asynchronous effects may all appear in a short routine.
Keep uncertainty searchable and local with `@todo` comments for suspected inlines rather than prematurely naming or refactoring the pattern.
naming_conventions: Unidentified Yoshi init functions retained address-based names such as `ftYs_Init_8012BDA0`, `ftYs_Init_8012BE3C`, `ftYs_Init_8012B804`, and `ftYs_Init_8012B918`.

Lifecycle callbacks use the `ftYs_Init_On...` naming pattern, for example `ftYs_Init_OnDeath`, `ftYs_Init_OnLoad`, `ftYs_Init_OnItemPickup`, `ftYs_Init_OnKnockbackEnter`, and `ftYs_Init_OnKnockbackExit`.
Yoshi neutral-special globals use `ftYs_UnkN_address` placeholder naming, e.g. `ftYs_Unk1_803B75C0`, until semantics are known.
The PR uses typed aliases where available: `Fighter_GObj*` for `ftYs_Init_8012BDA0`, `HSD_GObj*` for `ftYs_Init_8012BE3C`, `Fighter* fp`, `Fighter_Part part`, and `HSD_JObj* jobj`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched `ftYs_Init_8012BDA0` by reproducing a nested stack layout with padding and a local `struct UNK_SAMUS_S2` before calling collision setup.

Matched `ftYs_Init_8012BE3C` with a likely inline-sensitive sequence: store `xBC = &fp->co_attrs.xBC`, resolve part 4 through `ftParts_8007500C`, load `fp->parts[part].joint`, then call `efAsync_Spawn` with effect ID `1231`.
The asm refresh shows conversion from scratch-local addresses/labels to actual DOL addresses and call displacements, useful for verifying which functions are now represented by C.

The PR body report recorded many Yoshi init routines as 100% individually while the file-level report was 85.02%, indicating remaining mismatch or unaccounted bytes at file scope despite many matched functions.
Formatting changes in unrelated files appear incidental and should not be treated as semantic matching tactics.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or type `struct UNK_SAMUS_S2` after its use in `ftYs_Init_8012BDA0`.
Search for follow-up PRs that resolve the `/// @todo Some kind of inline here.` comment in `ftYs_Init_8012BE3C`.
Check whether `ftYs_Unk1_803B75C0` later receives a semantic name beyond the placeholder Yoshi unknown-vector name.
Compare subsequent `ftYs_Init.c` reports to see what remained after the 85.02% file-level match.
Search for other fighters using `ftColl_8007B5AC` with a temporary descriptor to reuse this stack-layout matching pattern.
